lernziele:
The students learn:

  • to distinguish between different ways of financing
  • to quantify the cost of capital and to optimize the capital structure of a company
  • how to make capital budgeting decisions
  • how to compute the value of bonds, shares and companies
  • to analyse financial reports with financial figures for multinational companies
  • to understand how financial reports in an international environment are prepared
  • how financial positions in the financial report are valued
  • the basics of business combinations (M&A)

kurse.lernziele:
Professional competences:

Participants can:

  • describe how financing, the cost of capital and the value of the company are interrelated.
  • explain the purpose and content of a financial report.
  • can explain and perform a business combination.
  • assess a financial analysis of a company.

 

Methodological competences:

Participants can:

  • explain the impact of different types of financing on the cost of capital and the capital structure
    policy.
  • apply the time value of money concept in the context of capital budgeting decisions and the
    valuation of bonds, shares and companies.
  • explain the content and the structure of an income statement, balance sheet and cashflow
    statement.
  • quantify the valuation of inventories, long-lived assets, deferred taxes and provisions.
  • assess the financial reporting quality.
  • assess the financial performance with financial ratios and financial reporting quality.
  • explain and apply business combinations (financial instruments, goodwill, equity accounting).
  • describe the consequences of multinational operations in the financial report.

 

Self-competences:

Participants can:

  • reflect the own results and the opinion of other students.
  • challenge the opinion of other students.

 

Social competences:

Participants can:

  • reflect the results and opinion of other students
kurse.planinhalt:
Topic area I: Capital Budgeting and Valuation

  • Time value of money
  • Value of bonds and equities
  • Capital budgeting
  • Corporate valuation

 

Topic area II: Corporate Financing

  • Debt and equity capital
  • Capital raising
  • Cost of capital
  • Capital structure policy

 

Topic area III: Financial Reporting

  • Introduction to financial reporting standards
  • Introduction to income statements, balance sheets and cash flow statements
  • Financial analysis technique

 

Topic area IV: Application of Financial Reporting Standards

  • Inventories
  • Long-lived assets
  • Deferred taxes
  • Long-term liabilities
  • Provisions

 

Topic area V: Business Combinations & Financial Analysis

  • Equity accounting, goodwill acccounting, financial instruments
  • Multinational accounting
  • Financial ratios
  • Financial reporting quality
kurse.empfLehrmittel: Front-of-class teaching, group work, case studies, exercises in class

pruefung.bemerkungPruefung:
1st part Corporate Finance, 2nd part Accounting

Closed book (a cheat sheet with own notes in the size of one A4 is allowed, written on both
sides), examination 2 hours (120 points, 1 minute = 1 point)
